title: Checking if SMPT implementation is good to move forward with
description:
The Johnston team is using Gravity Form for their marketing/community page, and while submissions are being captured correctly, the notification emails don’t appear to be coming through reliably. 
 
Brad would like to implement SMTP (via Post SMTP) on our WordPress instance to improve deliverability and make sure notifications are consistently received.
 
The plan would be to route outgoing mail through our Microsoft 365 SMTP (smtp.office365.com) using authenticated sending and a consistent “From” address (welcome@johnston-lawfirm.com), and to enforce that at the application level to avoid any plugin or theme overrides.
 
Before moving forward, Brad would like to double-check this aligns with anything already in place or any preferences you have around email handling.

description:
SEO

Campaign schedule
Weeks 1-3: Creative Ideation & Idea Viability Check (completed)
Weeks 3-5: Data Collection and Story Production
Weeks 5-8: Outreach and Pitching

Onsite SEO
- Onpage optimizations
- site structure changes or adaptations
- Internal linking updates
- Content provisions or changes (targeted, not random blogging)
- AI analysis recommendations fulfilled (content gaps, content focus, Key seo zones, internal link equity, on-site modifications that come out of ongoing R&D

title: Car accident lawyer keyword ranking
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Clayton Coombs, Corey V
description:
Discussion: How can we gain on car accident lawyer keyword - , please review Rob Klein's website and the strategy Mockingbird is employing. Rob Klein is an attorney who also works with them but they don't want to see his website being able to pull leads the firm should have. 

Here are the Ai notes from the discussion Corey had w/ Bill about car accident lawyer: 
    • Johnston Law average rank 7.8 for car accident terms
      • Johnston Law's average rank of 7.8 for car accident terms indicates a competitive presence in the market.
        This ranking suggests that the firm is effectively optimizing its content and SEO strategies to attract potential clients searching for car accident legal services.
      • Improving this rank further could lead to increased visibility and a higher volume of inquiries for car accident cases.
      • Rob Klein's website unexpectedly outperforming

      • Rob Klein's website has a high domain authority due to its long-standing presence and relevance in the cycling community.

      • The website's SEO strategy includes keyword stuffing in titles, which boosts its visibility in search rankings.

      • There is concern about the website attracting potential clients away from Johnston Law Firm, as Rob Klein receives a percentage from cases originated through his site.

      • Keyword stuffing in titles contributing to Rob's performance
        • Keyword stuffing in titles can lead to improved search engine rankings by increasing relevance for specific search terms.
        • This practice can enhance visibility in search results, attracting more clicks and traffic to the website.
        • However, excessive keyword stuffing may negatively impact user experience and lead to penalties from search engines if deemed manipulative.
        • Need to build more authority around car accident terms
        • Building authority around car accident terms can be achieved through the following strategies:
          Create high-quality, informative content that addresses common questions and concerns related to car accidents, such as legal rights, insurance claims, and safety tips.
          Engage in local SEO practices by optimizing website content for relevant keywords, ensuring accurate business listings, and gathering positive reviews from clients.
          Establish backlinks from reputable sources, such as legal blogs, news articles, and local business directories, to enhance credibility and improve search engine rankings.

title: CopyCat Notice
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Clayton Coombs, Corey V, Fed
description:
Hi guys! 

Johnston Law Firm received a copycat notification and asked us: 

Hi Team,
 
Wondering if you all have the proof of getting the referenced image from Shutterstock or similar so we can make these folks go away.

I believe this was around the time of our transition away from Nifty to Clixsy so I am not sure which agency posted the referenced page/photo.
 
Also, this could be a total scam, and I am just not sure.   Any help you could provide will be appreciated.
 
Thanks!
*Link to the email in basecamp if you rather read it that way. *

Sent via email to marc@johnston-lawfirm.com on September 25, 2024
Authorization request / Unauthorized image use - Case no: C86EC3
Dear Johnston Law Firm, P.C.,
We, COPYTRACK, are writing to you on behalf of our client YayImages, who has assigned us the monitoring and protection of their licenses and image rights globally. On September 24, 2024 we have been informed that Johnston Law Firm, P.C. is likely using an image without permission and the client has exclusively commissioned us with the clarification, administration of the image rights and, if necessary, the enforcement of any copyright infringement through our partner lawyers. Images are protected by copyright law and infringements are actionable under international law and the respective national law. Please see the attachment below for details.
On behalf of our client, we must first determine if you have a valid license to use the images in question. If you have a valid license or any other legal justification to use these images, please reply to this email and include proof of license purchase and/or any other necessary information to validate the usage.

Please note that you are obliged to provide the necessary information about the usage of the image to allow us to verify the lawfulness of such usage.

Please respond to this letter no later than October 5, 2024.

If this is not the case, your use of the image material most likely constitutes a copyright infringement. If no settlement can be reached, we as Copytrack are directly instructed to enforce these rights for the territory in the Federal Republic of Germany through our German partner lawyers and, in addition, to instruct our respective partner lawyers in other countries to examine and, if necessary, enforce the infringement of the rights in the respective countries. You would then be legally obligated to compensate our client for the damage caused by this copyright infringement.
Rights holder | YayImages
Image found on | https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-to-have-fun-and-play-it-safe-during-portlands-world-naked-bike-ride/
Online Since | December 21, 2023
3 steps to solving your caseIn order to resolve this case amicably with you, we request your cooperation. Please complete the following steps to close this case:1.   CheckReview the evidence on this case by going to https://portal.copytrack.com and entering CASE ID C86EC32.   ProofShow us proof of your license by uploading it or providing any other legal justification to use these images and if it is valid, we will close your case immediately;If you do not have a valid license or any other valid justification:3.   Compensation / LicenseYou now have the following 2 options:o  Option 1: Purchase of a subsequent image license (includes past and future use for one year after purchase).o  Option 2: Compensation (includes the previous use and requires the immediate deletion of the image).
Image license (valid for 1 year from date of purchase) | 978.09 €
Compensation costs (past usage) | 900.00 €
Payment is due by | October 24, 2024Payments for both options can be made securely online at https://portal.copytrack.com by entering CASE ID C86EC3.

We accept PayPal, credit card and bank transfer.

We calculate these fees based on our client's license history, as well as the duration of use and type of rights infringement. Failure to provide a proof or pay for a valid license may result in your case being referred to our partner attorney and significantly higher additional fees.

To avoid further action, including legal action, provide proof of a valid license or any other relevant information by October 5, 2024, or acquire it by making payment in our online portal.
Sincerely yours,
Marcus Schmitt

title: Reporting Call Prep
description:
This task must be fully updated by EOD Monday before the reporting call.

This document serves as:
  • Internal alignment between AM, CM, and SEO
  • Source of truth for call structure
  • Risk detection tool
  • Talking point preparation
No one should walk into the reporting call unprepared.

This prep aligns directly with the live call agenda.

AM should use this document to guide the call in real time.

No one should enter the call without this fully filled out.

SECTION 1: OPENING FRAME (AM)


What tone are we setting?

Client temperature this month:
 ☐ Strong
 ☐ Neutral
 ☐ Slightly concerned
 ☐ Frustrated

Notes from weekly pulse:


Any relationship items to acknowledge at opening?

Opening focus reminder:
What do we want the client to feel by minute 3?

SECTION 2: PERFORMANCE OVERVIEW (CM SECTION)


  please provide by Wednesday 12 PM:
  1. How is performance this month?
  2. Campaign health status: Green / Yellow / Red
  3. Areas of concern
  4. Anything stand out? Wins or things we need to be aware of?
  5. Do you need anything from the client?
  6. What is our main focus next month?
AM must review and confirm alignment with CM before call.

Risk Flag:
 ☐ No performance concerns
 ☐ Minor fluctuation
 ☐ Underperformance risk

If risk exists:
  • Has CM been aligned?
  • Has AM Lead been notified?
No surprises allowed on call.

SECTION 3: PERFORMANCE Q&A PREP


List anticipated performance questions:




Confirm:

☐ CM prepared for these
☐ Messaging aligned
☐ Time boundary plan set

SECTION 4: WORK COMPLETED (AM SECTION)


This mirrors the “Campaign Execution” portion of the call.

List in client-friendly language:
  • SEO deliverables completed:

  • Content published:

  • Links built:

  • Technical fixes:

  • Dev completed:

  • Wins (Sentinel, visibility gains, authority growth):

No internal jargon.

SECTION 5: WORK IN PROGRESS (AM)


  • Active SEO initiatives:

  • Content pipeline:

  • Technical initiatives:

  • Dev in progress:

  • Strategic pushes:

Must clearly show momentum.

SECTION 6: TEAM NEEDS (AM)


Do we need anything from the client?
  • Approvals:

  • Content:

  • Access:

  • Decisions:


If nothing needed, state clearly: None at this time.

SECTION 7: MONTH AHEAD VISION (AM)


This section drives confidence.

Answer clearly:
  • What is our main focus next month?
  • What are we monitoring closely?
  • What would success look like by next reporting call?

SECTION 8: FINAL Q&A PREP (AM)


Non-performance questions the client may raise:


Plan response or follow-up pathway.

Confirm:

☐ If unsure, defer and follow up
☐ CM will not re-enter unless pre-discussed

SECTION 9: ESCALATION CHECK


Before call, confirm:

☐ SEO updates received
☐ Dev updates confirmed
☐ Any blockers escalated
☐ AM Lead notified if risk exists
☐ CM aligned on any sensitive topics

If any box cannot be checked, escalate before call.

SECTION 10: CALL CONTROL REMINDERS (AM)


  • Set agenda at beginning
  • Protect time at minute 20
  • Transition clearly
  • Do not guess
  • Close confidently

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1:

Witness statements can have a major impact on your Oregon car accident claim, sometimes in ways that work against you. While witnesses are often seen as neutral, their accounts may be incomplete, inconsistent, or influenced by limited visibility, stress, or assumptions made at the scene. Insurance companies frequently rely on these statements to shift blame, reduce settlement offers, or dispute liability under Oregon’s comparative negligence rules.

If a witness suggests you were partially at fault, even inaccurately, it could directly affect your compensation. That’s why early legal review and investigation matter. An experienced attorney can analyze credibility, challenge flawed testimony, and present the full picture of what really happened.

If you were injured in a car accident, Johnston Law Firm is ready to protect your rights and your recovery.

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-witness-testimony-can-negatively-influence-car-accident-case/


=======================

POST #2:

Most car accident cases in Oregon settle out of court, but when insurers dispute fault or refuse fair compensation, litigation may be the next step. Going to court can feel overwhelming, especially when you’re already dealing with injuries, medical bills, and missed work. The good news is that court is a structured process, not a punishment, and preparation makes all the difference.

From filing the lawsuit and exchanging evidence to mediation, trial, and verdict, each phase is designed to clarify the facts and hold the right parties accountable. Many cases still settle during litigation once insurers see the case is trial-ready.

If your car accident claim may be headed to court, Johnston Law Firm is prepared to guide you through every stage and protect your rights.

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/what-happens-when-my-car-accident-case-goes-to-court/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #01

After a car accident, conflicting stories can quickly complicate an already stressful situation. That’s why police reports play such an important role in Oregon auto accident claims. A police report creates an official, third-party record of what happened while details are still fresh, helping protect you from false statements or shifting blame by the other driver.

At Johnston Law Firm, we regularly see how a well-documented police report can strengthen an injury claim by supporting your version of events, identifying witnesses, and noting early signs of injury or unsafe driving. Even when an accident seems minor, calling the police can prevent major problems later—especially if injuries develop or the other driver changes their story.

If you’re unsure how a police report affects your case or need help correcting inaccuracies, our team is here to guide you every step of the way.

#CarAccidentOregon #PoliceReportMatters #JohnstonLawFirm #PersonalInjuryHelp

Learn more:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/when-is-a-police-report-required-after-a-car-accident-in-oregon/


====================

POST #02

Being injured in a hit-and-run accident can leave you feeling overwhelmed, angry, and unsure of what to do next. In Portland, these crashes happen far too often, and victims are left dealing with injuries, insurance questions, and emotional stress after the responsible driver flees the scene.

At Johnston Law Firm, we help hit-and-run victims understand their rights and pursue compensation, even when the at-fault driver is never identified. Oregon law allows injured drivers to seek recovery through Uninsured Motorist coverage for medical bills, lost wages, pain and suffering, and more.

Taking the right steps early, in other words, reporting the crash, seeking medical care, and protecting your claim, can make a major difference. You don’t have to face insurance companies or legal uncertainty alone. Our team is here to provide clarity, guidance, and strong advocacy when you need it most.

#HitAndRunAccident #PortlandPersonalInjury #JohnstonLawFirm #CarAccidentHelp

Learn more:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/legal-guide-for-portland-victims-injured-in-hit-and-run-accidents/



====================

POST #03

Motorcycle accidents often result in serious injuries, but insurance companies frequently undervalue these claims. Riders are unfairly judged based on stereotypes, with insurers assuming reckless behavior or “accepted risk,” even when another driver clearly caused the crash. These biases can lead to reduced settlements that fail to account for long-term medical care, lost income, or lasting pain.

At Johnston Law Firm, we understand how insurers minimize motorcycle injury claims by downplaying severity, disputing fault, or pressuring riders into quick, low offers. Oregon’s comparative negligence rules are often used to shift blame onto riders, further reducing compensation. Strong medical evidence, clear documentation, and experienced legal advocacy are essential to counter these tactics.

If you’re being given the runaround after a motorcycle accident, you don’t have to face it alone. We fight to ensure riders are treated fairly and fully compensated for what they’ve endured.

#MotorcycleAccident #OregonInjuryLaw #JohnstonLawFirm #InsuranceDisputes

Learn more:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/why-insurance-undervalues-most-motorcycle-accident-injuries/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1 - December 2025

Distracted driving cases today rely on more than witness statements—cell phone data can play a critical role in revealing what really happened before a crash. Modern smartphones record detailed activity logs, including calls, texts, app usage, and screen interactions. When analyzed correctly, this data can help establish whether a driver was distracted at the moment of impact. In accident cases, even a few seconds of phone use can significantly affect liability and compensation.

Attorneys often combine cell phone records with crash reports, expert analysis, and physical evidence to create a clear timeline of events. Acting quickly is essential, as phone carriers may only retain records for a limited time. With experienced legal guidance, digital evidence can shift a case from speculation to proof and strengthen negotiations with insurers. If distracted driving is suspected, early legal involvement helps preserve critical evidence and protect your claim.

#DistractedDriving #CarAccidentLaw #PersonalInjuryAttorney #OregonAccidents

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-cell-phone-data-can-reveal-distracted-driving-in-an-accident-case/


===========================================

POST #2 - December 2025

After a car accident in Oregon, recovery often involves more than medical care; it also means dealing with insurance companies and detailed paperwork. One of the most important steps in this process is writing a clear, well-supported demand letter. This document outlines how the accident happened, the injuries you suffered, your medical treatment, financial losses, and the compensation you’re seeking.

When done correctly, a demand letter sets the tone for negotiations and shows insurers that your claim is serious and well-documented. Strong demand letters rely on facts, an organized structure, and supporting evidence like medical records, wage statements, and repair estimates. Avoid exaggeration, stay professional, and clearly explain how the accident has impacted your life. If you’re unsure where to start, legal guidance can help ensure your demand letter fully reflects your damages and protects your rights.

#OregonCarAccident #AccidentDemandLetter #PersonalInjuryLaw #InsuranceClaims #AccidentRecovery

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-to-write-an-oregon-car-accident-demand-letter/

Note: This blog is scheduled to go live on 12/15/2025.


===========================================

POST #3 - December 2025

When the other driver lies about a car accident, it can quickly complicate insurance claims and delay your recovery. False statements are more common than many drivers realize and often stem from fear of higher premiums, legal consequences, or simple panic. The most important step is protecting your claim with clear, objective evidence.

Take photos of the scene, vehicle damage, and road conditions, call the police, and gather witness information whenever possible. Avoid arguing at the scene or discussing fault with insurers before understanding your rights. Insurance companies rely on evidence, not just stories, when accounts conflict, but they may still try to minimize payouts. That’s where legal guidance can make a difference. An experienced attorney can gather evidence, challenge false claims, and ensure the truth is clearly presented so your rights and compensation are protected.

#CarAccidentHelp #PersonalInjuryLaw #InsuranceDisputes #OregonAccidents

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/what-to-do-if-the-other-driver-lies-about-the-car-accident/

Note: This blog post is scheduled to go live on 12/20/2025

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

description:
The H1 tag on the homepage: https://johnston-lawfirm.com/ is below the H2 and H3 tags, appearing in the "results" carousel.


Ideally, <h1>Oregon Personal Injury Lawyer</h1> should be above all the text elements on the page, including the <p></p> text in the main banner.

If this is not possible without major changes to the page template, here are two other possible solutions:

1. Set all the elements in the "We Get Results" carousel to <p></p>, but keep their size and styling.
2. Move the carousel below the "Oregon Personal Injury Lawyer" section.

title: GBP posts
description:
These are DEFINITELY posts for November! 😅

POST #01

Speeding turns a semi-truck into a dangerous projectile. Even small increases in speed drastically lengthen stopping distances, reduce reaction time, and amplify crash force. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we investigate truck accidents across Oregon to prove how speed changed everything. Our attorneys secure black box data, dashcam footage, and expert analysis to hold drivers and trucking companies accountable for reckless behavior.

When a speeding truck causes serious injury or wrongful death, insurance companies often downplay the role of velocity. We counter with facts, physics, and evidence. If you or a loved one were harmed in a truck crash, don’t face the aftermath alone. Contact our Portland-based team today for a free consultation and experienced legal guidance.

#TruckAccidentAttorney #OregonLaw #PersonalInjuryLawyer #SpeedingTruckCrash

Learn More:

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-a-speeding-semi-truck-magnifies-the-severity-of-crashes/


-----------------------------------------------------------------

POST #02

Employer pressure is a hidden cause behind many speeding truck crashes. Unrealistic delivery schedules, per-mile pay, and strict scorecards push drivers to rush, turning safe rigs into deadly projectiles. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we uncover the systemic pressure behind reckless speed and hold every responsible party accountable under Oregon law.

Our Portland-based attorneys gather telematics data, dispatch messages, and maintenance records to prove that company policies (not just driver error caused the crash. When trucking companies prioritize deadlines over safety, innocent motorists pay the price. If you or a loved one were hurt by a speeding semi-truck, contact Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm today for a free, confidential consultation. We fight for full compensation and safer roads for every Oregon family.

#TruckAccidentAttorney #OregonLaw #PersonalInjuryLawyer #SpeedingTruckCrash

Learn More:

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/how-employer-pressure-leads-to-speeding-truck-crashes/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #01:

Insurance companies often use denial, delay, and lowball tactics to minimize payouts after a fatal truck accident. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we recognize these strategies and counter them with evidence, expert testimony, and trial readiness. Our Portland-based wrongful death lawyers know that insurers protect their bottom line, not your family’s future. That’s why we move quickly to secure truck data, witness statements, and safety records that reveal the truth behind the crash.

Losing a loved one in a truck accident is devastating, but you don’t have to face the legal and financial aftermath alone. We help Oregon families demand accountability, full compensation, and justice under the law.

#WrongfulDeathLawyer #TruckAccidentAttorney #OregonLaw #PersonalInjuryJustice

Learn More:

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/insurance-tactics-in-wrongful-death-truck-accident-cases/



---------------------------------------

POST #02:

Proving negligence is the foundation of any wrongful death claim after a fatal car accident. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we help families across Oregon uncover the truth behind deadly crashes and hold negligent drivers accountable. Our attorneys gather critical evidence (police reports, black box data, expert testimonies, etc.) to establish fault and secure justice for your loved one.

Negligence cases demand precision and compassion. Whether the crash involved distracted driving, speeding, or intoxication, our Portland-based legal team builds every claim with care and strength. We understand both the emotional toll and the legal complexity of these tragedies. If you’ve lost a loved one in a fatal car accident, contact us today for a free consultation and let us fight for the accountability your family deserves.

#WrongfulDeathLawyer #CarAccidentAttorney #OregonLaw #JusticeForFamilies

Learn More:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/proving-negligence-in-fatal-car-accidents/

title: GBP posts
description:
POST #1:

The Oregon Department of Transportation is updating the Transportation Safety Action Plan, a 20-year roadmap aimed at reducing crashes and eliminating serious injuries. Running from 2026 through 2046, the plan will guide how agencies invest in proven safety strategies statewide. At Johnston Law Firm, we closely follow these updates because they shape how motor vehicle accidents are prevented, investigated, and resolved.

The new plan emphasizes data-driven investments, the Safe System approach, and stronger protections for vulnerable road users like pedestrians and cyclists. These changes matter for every Oregonian, from daily commuters to families seeking answers after collisions. If you’ve been injured in a car accident, our attorneys are here to provide guidance, pursue fair compensation, and help you move forward.

#OregonRoadSafety #CarAccidentLawyer #VisionZero #TransportationSafety

Learn more:
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/transportation-safety-action-plan-updates-for-2026/


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

POST #2:  Publish After 09/23/2025

Oregon’s 2025 legislative session brought major labor and employment law changes, with more taking effect in 2026. From wage liability on construction projects to limits on asking applicants’ age, new BOLI rules, unemployment benefits for striking workers, and sick leave use for blood donation, employers and employees alike need to prepare.

At Johnston Law Firm, we break down what these updates mean in plain English and track how they affect workplace disputes, hiring, and collective bargaining. While these laws don’t alter workers’ compensation directly, they reshape the broader employment landscape and influence how claims unfold. If you’ve suffered a workplace injury or face an employment-related dispute, our attorneys are here to protect your rights and secure fair results.

 #OregonEmploymentLaw #WorkplaceRights #LaborLaw #WorkersCompensation

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/new-oregon-labor-and-employment-laws-in-2025-and-2026/

Note: The article above is scheduled to go live on 09/23/2025.

title: Johnston Law: Monthly Reporting Call Prep
description:
BrightLocal Reports:
  • https://www.local-marketing-reports.com/location-dashboard/c347284c48f5ab9bd7df58080cc791b910f104ae/lsg/view?reportId=173679&keyword=personal+injury+attorney&runId=0194833b-df61-712b-8484-7e63c8fc7879&timeline=allTime
LSA/Smart Ads:
  • , please add the report for today's call
Questions + Needs:

Completed:

Addressing the Elephant in the room (refer to the ping I sent you):
  • More Content Collaboration: He’d love to be involved in the work and have the opportunity to give his approval, like clicking a friendly 'approve' button. We can use a content sheet and tell him we will start sending him the weekly pulse if he wants.
  • Mock Courtroom Content Initiative:  He’s looking to add a section on the homepage or create a landing page aimed at attorneys about using their mock courtroom. Kindly share your thoughts on this and how we can best proceed. Fed has already done something similar, but having this on record would be helpful.
  • Instagram-like Page Initiative: Brad has an idea to create a landing page to help direct traffic from Instagram. We’ve mentioned that we need to confirm with everyone if this aligns with our goals for the SEO. Let’s chat about this to ensure we’re all on the same page regarding our scope of work, since handling the Instagram strategy.

title: LSA/Smart Ad Spend
description:
Hi David, 
 The new CMO Brad requested if there's a way for him to see or get the past year's historical spend report for LSA/Smart ads?

title: Link goes to a 404
description: When I was performing a check on the site for dev's PHP update, I noticed this link on the truck accident page goes to a 404 page. Not sure if it matters, but thought I should check with you.

title: Update Theme to PHP 8.2
description:
Look into what it will take to update the theme to support PHP 8.2

https://johnston-lawfirm.clixsysites.com/

We want to start with an estimate of time.  I know that can be very hard without actually jumping into the project.

If the hours is excessive we may want to bring this up with the client manager.  We may want to make a recommendation for an alternative solution.

title: Question from Mark/ fed to update team
description:
Bill emailed in a question from Mark and I'd like some help in answering this, as I'm not sure if Corey's had a chance to review it: 

  1. On the homepage, when you hover over the “locations” tab it lists 6 cities.  That could be very misleading to potential clients from throughout Oregon.  And of course, we serve the entire state.  At the bare minimum, when we hover over the locations tab there needs to be a tab that says “Oregon” or something signifying that we serve more than those 6 cities.

How do you guys want me to respond to Bill which in turn would update Mark.

title: Photo's on page covering text
assignees: Andrei Pocol, Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss
description:
Hey so I was just reviewing the car accident page on johnston-lawfirm.com
https://johnston-lawfirm.com/car-accident/

Can we create a task to clean these images up, so they do not cover text.

Also maybe we can take a look at other pages as well.

Another thing I noticed is there is no TOC on the page, i wonder if we would want one on the case type pages.

title: Normal to see rotating numbers?
assignees: Clayton Coombs, Corey V, Keith Bruss
description:

Notes from Andrei

Analytics Summary

https://docs.google.com/document/d/19KmnagEAHxbARmdiSoWbAHLHtGxiqi9rTclpQjb0x6s/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.jq1qcgwhzod7 

The statistics in AHREFs look unfavorable, but this seems to be a common trend for nearly every website I’ve checked with AHREFs in the past few months. However, Google Search Console (GSC) and Bright Local data appear acceptable. On the other hand, GA4 is showing a decline in traffic acquisition over the past 30 days compared to the previous 30 days, likely due to issues with the Google Business Profile (GBP), as detailed below. Thus, the GA4 data presented in the report reflects only the traffic acquisition difference from one year ago.

Additionally, it seems there is an ongoing issue with the GBP. Apparently, related keywords have been removed, and the brand name is now listed as "Johnston Law Firm" instead of "Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm." While changes will probably be made, I do not handle any modifications to GBPs; I believe Mikey takes care of that.

If the brand name for the GBP is updated to "Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm," we will also revise the text throughout the website to ensure consistency.  Have you seen anything on your end regarding Andrei's GBP note?

Moreover, with this campaign, we've lost a significant number of clicks on branded keywords, such as "Johnston Law Firm" and other variations that include "Johnston." The homepage has seen the largest decline in clicks. This issue may be attributed to the GBP problem, along with other factors as well. I believe Mubeen has prepared a more detailed report regarding this matter.

    Daniel and i wanted to touch base on this today but haven't been able to sync up just yet: 
     
    • I thought I would respond on the email that you were referring to for ease of information. This estimate of work is very high level. When we meet with Marc he’ll have questions that I’m sure you’ll be able to answer but I wanted to have some more detail before that happens. Here are some examples.

      Website Refresh & SEO adjustment:
      ● Website Refresh: $6500.00-$8K ○ We truly need to identify our targets and goals before dialing in the amount – Agreed, what is possible for this amount? I know a first step was to do an inventory. Has that occurred? What do you need from me? Should I be responding to the inventory or supplying some kind of direction regarding what to focus on vs. what to de-prioritize? What are you guys recommending for increased performance?

       
       
      ● SEO: $15,000 (increased SEO budget from $10,750) ○ Objective: Increase SEO spend to rank Statewide for Truck Accident and adjust for Catastrophic or serious injuries
      ○ Strategy, Implementation, on-page optimization – What is needed here? I think of this as something that needs a marketing brief or a document that serves that purpose.
      ○ GBP Setup & Monthly Optimization – What do we need to improve?
      ○ Content creation, Directories & Authoritative Back Links – Examples? Ultimately you may be providing content, or me, or both of us. I’d like to have some idea of the “what”.
      ○ Quarterly Digital PR Campaign ■ Potential to add more ad hoc – Very high level. Is there a typical PR campaign process you follow?
       
      I  don’t need a polished deck. Just a little more information.
       
      In addition to whatever the monthly update tomorrow is, it would be wonderful to be able to say generally when we would be able to meet to discuss this additional work with Marc. Before that, I’d like to be smarter about what we intend to do.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Were You Injured as a Passenger in a Car Accident in Oregon? Know Your Rights!


Car accidents are tough enough, but when you're the passenger, it can feel even more overwhelming. You weren’t behind the wheel—so now what? In Oregon, passengers have the legal right to pursue compensation for injuries sustained in a crash. Whether you were riding with a friend, using a rideshare service like Uber or Lyft, or simply commuting with a family member, the law protects you.

You may be entitled to compensation for medical bills, lost wages, and emotional distress. But it’s not always simple—multiple insurance companies could be involved, and figuring out who’s liable can get complicated, especially in multi-vehicle or rideshare collisions.

That’s where Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm comes in. Our team has the experience to guide you through the process, protect your rights, and fight for the compensation you deserve. If you've been hurt as a passenger, don't delay—your time to file a claim is limited.

📞 Contact us today for a free consultation.

#PassengerRights #OregonInjuryLawyer #CarAccidentHelp #KnowYourRights

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/can-passengers-file-a-claim-after-a-car-accident-in-oregon/

description:
🚗 Were You Wrongly Blamed in a Car Accident? Here’s What You Can Do

Being involved in a car accident is stressful—but being wrongly blamed for it in a police report can make things even worse. In Oregon, police reports are often the first thing insurance companies look at when deciding fault and compensation. If your report contains errors or unfair conclusions, it could seriously affect your ability to recover damages for your injuries or vehicle.

Mistakes in police reports are more common than you'd think. Whether it’s incorrect facts, misquoted statements, or biased assumptions, these reports aren’t infallible—and they can be challenged. At Johnston Personal Injury Law, we help clients dispute inaccuracies and unfair fault findings, using evidence, legal expertise, and proven strategies to set the record straight.

Acting quickly is key. If you suspect errors in your report, don't wait. Let us help protect your rights.

📞 Free Consultations Available

#CarAccidentHelp #DisputePoliceReport #OregonLaw #PersonalInjuryAttorney

title: Add GBP Post
description:
🚗 Insurance Companies May Try to Deny Your Car Accident Claim – Know Your Rights! 🚗

After a car accident, you expect your insurance company to act in good faith—but many insurers prioritize minimizing payouts over protecting you. They may delay claims, downplay injuries, offer lowball settlements, or even deny claims without explanation.

Some common tactics include:
✅ Delaying the process to wear you down
✅ Questioning the severity of your injuries
✅ Shifting blame to reduce compensation
✅ Using social media monitoring to discredit your claim
✅ Pressuring you to settle quickly before consulting a lawyer

💼 If your claim has been delayed, denied, or undervalued, Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm can help. Our experienced attorneys know how to fight back and secure the compensation you deserve. Contact us today for a free consultation!

#CarAccidentLawyer #InsuranceClaimDenied #KnowYourRights #PersonalInjury #JohnstonLawFirm #FreeConsultation

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/tactics-insurance-companies-use-to-deny-car-accident-claims/

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Can Dashcam Footage Help Your Oregon Car Accident Case?

Dashcam footage can be crucial in proving fault after a car accident. It captures key details like reckless driving, road conditions, and the moment of impact. This evidence can help counter false claims, assist police reports, and support your case for compensation.

Oregon law has specific rules on dashcam placement and audio recording. If you have dashcam footage from your crash, consult an experienced car accident lawyer. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we analyze your footage, determine its legal value, and fight to maximize your compensation.

Don’t let your evidence go to waste—contact us today for a free consultation!

#CarAccidentLawyer #DashcamEvidence #OregonLaw #LegalHelp #FreeConsultation

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/the-importance-of-dashcam-footage-as-evidence-in-car-accidents/

200 SW Market St # 1900, Portland, OR 97201, United States

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Were You Partially at Fault for an Accident? You May Still Be Entitled to Compensation!

Many accident victims assume they cannot seek compensation if they were partially responsible for the incident. However, Oregon follows a modified comparative negligence rule, meaning you can still recover damages if you were less than 51% at fault. Your compensation will be reduced based on your percentage of liability, but you do not have to forfeit your right to recover.

Insurance companies often try to maximize your liability to minimize your payout. That is why it is crucial to work with an experienced personal injury attorney who can build a strong case on your behalf. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we will fight to protect your rights and pursue the compensation you deserve.

📞 Call us for a free consultation today!

#PersonalInjury #KnowYourRights #OregonLaw #JohnstonLawFirm

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/oregons-comparative-negligence-rule-and-its-impact-on-your-personal-injury-claim/

description:
In Oregon, the statute of limitations can make or break your personal injury or property damage case. At Johnston Law Firm, we want you to understand your rights—and protect them. Most personal injury claims, like car accidents or dog bites, have a strict 2-year filing window. Wrongful death cases allow 3 years, while property damage claims may give you up to 6 years. Workers’ compensation cases also come with their own deadlines. Missing any of these timelines could mean forfeiting your right to the compensation you deserve.

If you're unsure how the statute applies to your situation, don’t wait. Evidence fades and witnesses disappear. Let our experienced team guide you through every step. Contact us today for a free consultation.

#OregonInjuryLaw #KnowYourRights #StatuteOfLimitations #JohnstonLawFirm

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/oregons-statute-of-limitations-for-different-types-of-injury-claims/

200 SW Market St # 1900, Portland, OR 97201, United States

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Who’s Liable in a Bus Accident? Know Your Rights

Bus accidents can lead to severe injuries, medical expenses, and emotional distress. Determining liability depends on whether the accident involved a public transit bus or a private operator. Public buses, run by government agencies, have unique legal protections, making claims more complex. Strict deadlines, damage caps, and procedural requirements may apply.

Private bus companies, including charter and tour buses, must meet federal and state safety standards. Liability may fall on the bus driver, company, maintenance providers, or even third parties like other drivers or government agencies responsible for road maintenance.

At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we fight for victims’ rights, ensuring they receive the compensation they deserve. If you or a loved one has been injured in a bus accident, contact us today for a free consultation!

#BusAccident #PersonalInjuryLawyer #KnowYourRights #LegalHelp

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/who-is-liable-in-a-bus-accident-understanding-claims-against-public-and-private-transit-operators/

title: Add GBP Post
description:
After a car accident, dealing with the aftermath can feel overwhelming, especially when it comes to determining liability and securing fair compensation. Police reports play a vital role in this process, offering crucial details about the crash, such as date, time, location, witness information, and contributing factors like driver negligence or impairment. These reports are often used to strengthen your case, whether in negotiations with insurance companies or legal proceedings.

At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we are highly proficient at leveraging this evidence while conducting thorough investigations to support your claim. Whether your injuries are life-altering or minor, our attorneys are here to fight for the compensation you deserve. Contact us today to learn how we can help you move forward.

#CarAccidentLawyer #OregonLegalHelp #PersonalInjury #JohnstonLawFirm #FreeConsultation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Rideshare accidents can leave passengers and others wondering who’s liable. In Oregon, determining fault can be complex, with liability potentially involving the driver, rideshare company, or another party. Strict laws require rideshare companies to carry insurance for drivers, providing coverage in specific scenarios. However, navigating these policies and Oregon’s comparative negligence laws can be overwhelming.

At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, our experienced attorneys help untangle the complexities of rideshare accident cases, from investigating liability to proving your losses. Whether through negotiations or in court, we’re committed to fighting for the compensation you deserve. Don’t let the insurance companies dictate your recovery—trust us to advocate for your rights.

#RideshareAccidents #PortlandLegalHelp #PersonalInjuryLawyer #JusticeForVictims #FreeConsultation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Injured in a Portland Transit Bus Accident? Here's What to Do

Accidents involving Portland transit buses can be traumatic, leaving victims with serious injuries, emotional distress, and financial challenges. Knowing the steps to take after a bus accident is crucial for protecting your legal rights and securing fair compensation. Prioritize safety, seek medical attention, document the scene, and gather contact information from all involved parties and witnesses. These actions can make a significant difference in the outcome of your claim.

At Johnston Law Firm, we’re dedicated to helping Portland bus accident victims navigate the complex legal process. From investigating the accident to negotiating with insurance companies, our team fights for justice on your behalf.

Contact us today for a free consultation. Let us help you get back on track.

#BusAccidentLawyer #PortlandTransitAccident #PersonalInjuryHelp #JohnstonPersonalInjuryLawFirm
#FreeConsultation

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/what-to-do-after-an-accident-involving-a-portland-transit-bus/

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Being involved in a motor vehicle accident with a drunk driver can be life-altering. Insurance companies often try to reduce or deny claims to protect their profits, leaving victims to shoulder medical bills, property damage, and lost wages. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we know the tactics insurers use to minimize payouts. Our legal team is experienced in handling car accident claims, ensuring that victims of drunk drivers receive the maximum compensation they deserve. We manage the entire legal process—investigating the accident, gathering evidence, and negotiating with insurers—so you can focus on your recovery. If a drunk driver injured you or a loved one, don’t navigate the legal system alone. Schedule a free consultation today to learn your legal options and secure justice.

#DrunkDrivingAccident #PersonalInjuryLaw #LegalHelp #JohnstonLawFirm #FreeConsultation

Delivery truck accidents can cause devastating injuries due to their size and weight. If you’ve been in an accident with a delivery vehicle, determining liability isn’t always straightforward. Several parties could be responsible, including the driver, delivery company, maintenance team, or even the truck manufacturer. A thorough investigation is essential to establish fault and pursue fair compensation for medical expenses, lost wages, property damage, and more.

At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we have years of experience helping accident victims secure the justice they deserve. Our dedicated team provides personalized legal support and works tirelessly to build a strong case on your behalf. Contact us today for a free consultation and let us fight for the compensation you’re entitled to.

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Impaired driving laws in Oregon are evolving to combat DUII-related accidents and fatalities. The 2024 Impaired Driving Strategic Plan (IDSP) outlines goals to prevent and reduce impaired driving, focusing on education, enforcement, treatment, and legislation. From increasing high-visibility enforcement to supporting mandatory DUII treatment programs, the state is taking action to enhance road safety and hold impaired drivers accountable.

Oregon drivers should understand the IDSP to help support safer communities and make informed choices. If you’ve been injured in an accident caused by an impaired driver, you may face significant challenges. At Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm, we’re here to guide you through Oregon’s DUII laws and help you seek the compensation you deserve.

Contact us today to learn how we can assist with your case. Together, we can work toward justice.

#OregonDUII #RoadSafety #PersonalInjuryLawyer

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/oregons-2024-impaired-driving-strategic-plan-a-summary/

title: Add GBP Post
description:
🚶 Portland’s Vision Zero and the Ongoing Battle for Pedestrian Safety 🚦


Pedestrian and traffic accidents remain a pressing concern in Portland. Despite the Vision Zero initiative aiming to eliminate traffic fatalities, recent audits reveal critical gaps in its implementation. In 2023 alone, Portland recorded 69 traffic-related deaths, including 24 pedestrians—a stark reminder that safety measures still fall short.

Key challenges include insufficient lighting and crosswalks, staffing shortages, and outdated infrastructure. With 70% of traffic deaths between 2019 and 2022 occurring in low-light conditions, the need for improved illumination is clear. Other recommendations include expanding speed camera use and redesigning high-crash corridors to prioritize safety for all road users.

For those injured in traffic accidents, the struggle doesn’t end at recovery. Navigating medical bills, emotional distress, and insurance claims can be overwhelming. The Johnston Personal Injury Law Firm is here to help victims and their families secure the justice and compensation they deserve.

Let us help you focus on healing while we handle the legal complexities. 💼

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/audit-reveals-pbots-vision-zero-struggles-to-ensure-pedestrian-safety/

#PortlandPedestrianSafety #VisionZero #TrafficAccidentHelp #PersonalInjuryLawyer

200 SW Market St # 1900, Portland, OR 97201, United States

title: Add GBP Post
description:
The recent fatal bicycle accidents in Portland serve as a sobering reminder of the ongoing risks cyclists face on our city streets. Within a span of just 12 hours, two fatal crashes claimed the lives of cyclists, underscoring the urgent need for improved safety measures. Despite efforts by city officials to redesign hazardous intersections and install protected bike lanes, these tragic events highlight that more needs to be done to safeguard Portland’s cycling community.

The first incident occurred in a redesigned area that was supposed to improve safety, while the second took place in an industrial zone lacking proper bike lanes or shoulders. These accidents show the importance of continuing to push for safer infrastructure across Portland.

If you or a loved one has been affected by a bicycle accident, our experienced bicycle accident attorneys are ready to offer the guidance and support you need in seeking justice and compensation.

#BicycleSafety #PortlandCycling #CyclistRights #SafeStreets #BikeAccidentHelp #PortlandSafety #FreeConsultation

description:
Distracted driving poses a major threat to road safety in Oregon. From 2017 to 2021, over 26,000 accidents were caused by distracted drivers, resulting in 194 fatalities and thousands of injuries. Oregon’s distracted driving law strictly prohibits using handheld devices while driving, including talking, texting, or emailing. Violations can lead to hefty fines and, in severe cases, jail time. Exceptions exist, such as for emergency responders or voice-to-text for those over 18, but it’s best to keep your phone away on the road.

If you’ve been injured by a distracted driver, you have the right to seek compensation. At Johnston Law Firm, P.C., we’re dedicated to holding negligent drivers accountable. Our experienced legal team is ready to fight for you, ensuring you receive fair compensation for your injuries and losses. Call us today or fill out our contact form for a free case review.

description:
Riding a motorcycle through Oregon's beautiful landscapes is an exhilarating experience, but safety should always come first. Whether you're cruising along Crater Lake or heading to Cannon Beach, following the right safety practices can make all the difference. Wearing a DOT-compliant helmet, proper gloves, jacket, and boots could prevent severe injuries in case of an accident. It's also crucial to stay aware of Oregon’s motorcycle laws—such as avoiding lane splitting, which is illegal and could complicate your compensation claim if you're involved in an accident.

Make yourself visible on the road by riding in the center of your lane, wearing reflective gear, and using proper signals. Preparing for the worst, like carrying emergency contact information and a first-aid kit, can also save lives. If an accident occurs, consult a motorcycle attorney to ensure your legal rights are protected.

Stay safe, and enjoy Oregon's roads responsibly!

title: Add GBP Post
description:
With 6,950 courier and local delivery businesses operating in Oregon, delivery trucks are a common sight on our roads. While they provide valuable services, accidents involving these trucks can be complex. Knowing what to do after such an accident can protect your rights and maximize your chance of receiving compensation. If possible, move your vehicle out of traffic and turn on your flashers. Immediate medical care is crucial, even if you feel fine. Delayed symptoms from injuries like whiplash or concussions can appear later. Report the accident, especially if there are injuries or significant property damage. Take photos, videos, and collect information from the truck driver and any witnesses. Determining liability can be challenging. The delivery company, the truck driver, or another party may be responsible. Consulting an experienced Oregon truck accident lawyer is essential. At Johnston Law Firm, we rigorously investigate every case, ensuring your rights are protected. Learn more: https://johnston-lawfirm.com/what-to-do-after-an-accident-involving-a-delivery-truck/

200 SW Market St # 1900, Portland, OR 97201, United States

#TruckAccidentAttorney #oregon #personalinjury #freeconsultation

title: Add GBP Post
description:
Although manufacturers might be far from the accident scene, they can still be held responsible for any defects in their products. Various defects could have caused the truck accident, such as manufacturing defects, marketing defects, or design defects. If a specific make of truck has been recalled, it indicates that the manufacturer is aware of a defective part or engine malfunction. Legally, the manufacturer must attempt to reach all affected vehicles on the road. If the truck involved in your accident had a recalled part or was subject to a recall, the manufacturer and other distributors might be liable for failing to remove the truck from circulation. Learn more: lawfirm.com/manufacturer-liability-in-truck-accidents/
#truckaccidentattorney #personalinjury #oregon #freeconsultation

assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Unfortunately this has been labelled as urgent by the client as they are getting a large number of calls about it. So I have marked this as priority, ie something needs to be launched today. It's a new client and there have been one or two issues so we'd be as well going above and beyond for this client, but not ridiculously so. can possible define 'above and beyond' in the notes.

Please tell me first what you think can be accomplished today and in-scope. Here is the info from the client:

--------------

Nice to have: an animated pop-up window that has a button to exit the pop-up and goes away if someone wants to just scroll down the page.  
 
Sufficient: in-page disclaimer that stands out from the rest of the content at the top of the page that disappears as someone scrolls down the page.





Alert: Beware of Scam Solicitation from Johnston_Lawfirm@hotmail.com

 
It has come to our attention that there is a fraudulent scheme underway targeting individuals, seeking to exploit personal information under the guise of legitimate business correspondence. We want to ensure your safety and security by alerting you to this scam and providing necessary guidance to avoid falling victim to it.
 
The scam involves an email solicitation from the address Johnston_Lawfirm@hotmail.com, purportedly sent by someone claiming to be Marc Johnston from our firm. Please be advised that this email is NOT affiliated with Marc Johnston or our law firm in any capacity. It is a fraudulent attempt to deceive individuals into providing sensitive information, such as resumes, under false pretenses.
 
We want to stress the importance of vigilance in protecting your personal information online. Under no circumstances should you respond to this email or provide any information to the sender. Additionally, if you receive any text messages soliciting similar information, please disregard them and do not engage with the sender.
 
To reiterate, Johnston_Lawfirm@hotmail.com is not a legitimate email address associated with our firm. Any communication from this address should be treated with extreme caution and reported as fraudulent activity.
 
If you have already been in contact with the sender or have inadvertently provided any information, we strongly encourage you to take immediate action to safeguard your identity and finances. This may include contacting your financial institution, credit bureaus, and relevant authorities to report the incident and take necessary precautions.
 
Please rest assured that we are actively working to address this issue and prevent further dissemination of false information. The security and trust of our community and clients are of the utmost importance to us, and we are committed to maintaining the highest standards of integrity and professionalism.

title: Please update core values...
description:
Strictly speaking, probably out-of-scope but as it's a new client we should probably do it...

Hello!
Marc would like to update the “Core Values” on the website.  They are listed under careers.  If possible we would like them to be interactive-so perhaps you just see the core value and then you ‘click’ and somehow the rest is revealed. Is this possible?  I’ve attached the updated values.  Thank you so much!
 
Best, Paige

https://johnston-lawfirm.com/careers/



 

JLF new Core Values (Detailed) 


LIKE MAMA: 

We treat our clients the way a good mama treats her child. We go above and beyond for our clients at every step of the case. Our competitive advantage is deep care and concern for our clients. We strive relentlessly not just for case results, but for an incredible client experience and for the physical recovery of our clients. What can we do today to make our clients happy and show them we care? 

 

CLIENT RESULTS DRIVEN: 

We are driven to achieve the absolute best possible financial results for our clients in the shortest period of time. We celebrate great results! We say no to bad offers, file lawsuits, go to arbitration and go to trial when necessary to ensure the insurance company pays what they owe to our clients. Our drive to win for the client fuels us. Even after achieving great results, we diligently negotiate liens and balances to return maximum financial justice for our clients. 

TEAM FIRST: 

“Collaborative” is the #1 word our team uses to describe itself. This occurred intentionally, with an emphasis on creating a firm where people could both seek help, and were expected to help their co-workers.  New ideas from team members are not only appreciated, they are praised and frequently put into action. Firm culture balances hard work with fun and humor, and is always respectful and inclusive.  Our workplace is a drama free zone, with a palpable focus on positive thought and action. Happy people who prize a drama free workplace love working here! 

CREATIVITY: 

The law is many things, but practicing it is an art. Creativity is a major driver of financial justice for our clients, and leads to new approaches, innovation in the legal field and helps us resolve difficult cases and issues. Creative tactical approaches are prized and are seen as the building blocks of legal innovation and future firm success. 

PRACTICING EXCELLENCE: 

Our written and verbal work product, and our reputation with clients and within the legal community, is something we hold dear. It must never be sacrificed. We practice excellence in these areas regardless of whether it matters to the end result. We practice excellence because it matters to our soul. We practice excellence not only for our clients—we practice excellence for ourselves. 

BETTER THAN YESTERDAY: 

This firm’s past and its success is built on learning, becoming better and personal and professional growth. This means a driving force to improve, and the follow through to put in the time and the hard work necessary to become better than you once were. We do not rest on our past accomplishments, our education or our experience. That is merely a jumping off point to a lifetime of learning, discovering better methods and improving our craft. Better than yesterday is our past, and our future. At JLF, we see ourselves as always having more to learn, and not as a finished product.

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466

description:
Answer any questions and have the client sign the FCC during the Discovery Meeting.

Once again, record a copy of the signed FCC in the form field below.

title: Discovery Meeting
description:
Follow the Discovery Meeting Agenda prepared before.

Remember to ask for referrals!

Ask if they know anyone else you should talk to who is facing similar issues or runs a similar type of business.  Record any referred leads in the form field below.

Transfer all Referrals to CRM for follow up.

Referrals Contact Sheet uploaded here.

title: Email Discovery Meeting Agenda to client
Prepare Discovery Meeting Agenda
description:
DISCOVERY MEETING INTERNAL CHECKLIST


Have you discussed the Client's Business/Campaign Goals? One goal per campaign.

Have you built a Typical Customer Journey for the Client's business?

Have you built a Typical Buyer Persona for each campaign?

Have you decided on Campaign KPIs to measure our success?

Have you agreed on Monthly KPI Targets?

Have you set Campaign Budgets?

Have you spoken about common objections to sales the Client has experienced?

Have you done a SWOT analysis on each of the Client's Top 3 competitors?

Has Client intimated any legal constraints to advertising?

Have you discussed Integrated Marketing and looked at Client's past and current advertising campaigns?

Has the Client brought images/logos etc.?

Have you brainstormed ideas for creatives/messages/slogans/copy etc.?

Have you suggested any further services that could be beneficial to the Client?

If the CPC has changed have you transferred changes to Final Client Contract (FCC)?

Have you agreed the new Price & Payment Terms?

Have you written up the Final Client Contract (FCC) while Client is still in the office?

Have you got the Final Client Contract (FCC) signed?

Have you discussed incentives/discounts for referrals?

Have you asked for referrals? Add the on the next sheet.

title: Add agreed KPIs to top of Basecamp project
